Learning new practices.

Acquiring magic items.

Clues, representing nonspecific knowledge, inspiration, and half-formed thoughts on a field or broader subject.

title: Pact Dice — Downtime
excerpt:
Players will get three actions to conduct during ‘downtime’, those periods between sessions or adventures, and can choose actions from the lists below to fit into those slots, representing what they do when ‘offscreen’.

Most downtime

title: Pact Dice: Design — Skills

title: Pact Dice: Design — Backgrounds

The Mastery tree is representative of your limited time, energy, and focus, on a mundane, basic level. You’ll have a certain number of slots, and you fill them with beneficial things like:

Ongoing rituals or effects.

Things you’re

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — PACT DICE: Hosts
excerpt: Summons one or more Others who then occupy the practitioner or a part of the practitioner. May come with mutations or powers, or access to natural abilities and senses. May cause damage or miniscule long-term changes to the practitioner,

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Existence as a Host

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Basic Practice - Manifest

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Basic Practice - Invitation

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Advanced Use

Once finished, depending on contract and spirit, may allow keeping the Other on hand, as a token. See the Hoarde

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Basic Practice - Medium

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Example Uses…

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Basic Practicing - Cleanse, Bar, & Forfeit
excerpt: Not a practice, exactly, but a collection of means and protocols for the management of costs and penalties. In short, knowing this means the Practitioner can better manage the costs and penalties of long-term Hosting. They gain the

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Cleanse:

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Bar:
excerpt: In the face of any lingering physical, mental, or other changes, the practice of barring represents a resistance and fight against these changes. The body may be bound or decorated, or prepared in a more permanent way. Chains, strings of

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Forfeit:
excerpt: The opposite approach to the barring of a progressive change in oneself. To forfeit is to allow the change to happen, but to guide and negotiate it. The shape or location of any physical changes may be adjusted or minimized, and any loss

title: Pact Dice: Hosts — Basic Practice - Pound of Flesh

Care and Maintenance of Leaflight

The following tasks are your responsibility

title: Pact Dice: Astrologers — PACT DICE: Astrologers

excerpt: It’s fairly common for an Astrologer to have a set of maps in their office or sanctum, littered with symbols and lines, and, by a similar token, they might start a confrontation thinking or asking, “Which way is North?” or “Which direction

title: Pact Dice: Early Writeup of Alt/Expanded Ruleset
excerpt:

Practitioners have certain practices - spells, diagrams, items, and rituals that they can use to influence the world.

In Pact Dice, you’ll engage with other players and/or the GM in a ‘draft’ of your

excerpt:
The Cards for Family may be allocated to the domains of Birthright, Self, Establishment, and Affairs, and then one of House, Patron, or Discovery. In brief:

Birthright determines one’s resources, the value of things picked up from certain

title: Pact Dice (index) — PACT DICE

A practitioner should

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — PACT DICE: Collectors
excerpt: Collectors either don’t practice or practice less than the traditional practitioner. Their primary focus is in collecting things of a particular type; the greater the unification of the collection, the greater the collector, with larger &

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Existence as a Collector
excerpt: Collectors are typically inducted into their practice with the gift of an item, tying them into their family’s or mentor’s practice, their patron’s field of interest, or their patron’s field of Otherness. Wealthy and powerful families may

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Collectors & Types of Power
excerpt: Puissance is the cap on the practitioner’s ability to pull out power out of an item, both baseline and the advantages from diagrams. A high puissance collector is just better at getting the most out of magic items, and escalates fast as

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Example Sets
excerpt:
A ‘set’ is a particular arrangement with a benefit. Common examples:

A ‘streak’ of consecutive colors (red, orange, yellow) grants specific benefits to the collection, based on the orientation of the items and the colors in question. See

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Colors
excerpt:
Red is the color of the sword of war. Aggression, conflict, offensive uses.

Orange is the color of the sunrise and sunset and the explorer's torch. Horizons, travel, movement.

Yellow is the color of the cat's eyes, the moon, and the

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Endgame: Complete Collections
excerpt: The goal of practitioners trying to complete a true collection is typically to collect a large set of grails, where they are collecting ‘the’ items under a category. One couldn’t have a sword collection without Excalibur, for example.

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Some Relevant Others & others
excerpt: Peddlers - A broad grouping of Others with a knack for making or finding objects that have some power to them, which they then distribute. They may make, curse or tweak the items or manipulate the subjects so they destroy themselves in a

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Item Origins
excerpt:
Items may be:

Cursed; the reality is that many or even most ‘items’ that are put out there are released into the ‘wild’ or given to specific people for malicious reasons. They may have to be purified, or their drawbacks worked around. If

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Basic Practice - Bequeath
excerpt:
Collectors early in their careers will learn the powers that items hold and how to manipulate this power.

The Practice: With a bit of time and a few ritual words chosen by the practitioner and repeated each time this practice is used,

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Basic Practice - Transubstantiate
excerpt:
The conundrum of a collector is that it is hard to form a collection without the ability to find objects for the set, but finding objects in the set depends on having those objects to begin with.

Transubstantiate is an option that allows

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Basic Practice - Seize
excerpt: Collectors dwell on ownership, and in this world, the haves can have more, and the have nots are left in the cold. Rooted in this principle, this simple rite is used with surprising frequency when two collectors find themselves fighting

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Basic Practice - Gimcracks and Trifles
excerpt:
Collectors will often focus on one school of things, but the world is filled with things of the most minor practice or Otherness, and these things may be meant to be owned or used. A simple ritual that opens the doors.

The Practice: A

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Basic Practice - Draw Out
excerpt:
Collectors often find themselves in conflict with the practitioners or Others who covet what they have, or who have what they covet. Should things come to blows, it is nice to have one trick up one’s sleeve. Or a weapon.

The Practice: An

title: Pact Dice: Collectors — Types of Collector
excerpt: Wild Collectors are more ‘classic’ Collectors, creating a collection with a loose intent or even changing their intent based on what ends up in their collection and what makes the best sets. Flexible, but progression may be halting or

title: Pact Dice: Heartless — PACT DICE: Heartless
excerpt: Heartless give up their body. The inverse of the Host, they work to make themselves the possessing force. They are the liches, the hags, the parasite forces, and more, often losing the ability to practice over time or over generations as

title: Pact Dice: Heartless — Existence as a Heartless
excerpt:
Heartless, more than any other practice, have a marked progression between beginners, intermediate practitioners, and veteran practitioners.

As beginners, a heartless typically has a mentor, while full-fledged families are somewhat rare.

title: Pact Dice: Heartless — Types of heartless
excerpt: Heart in a Box - The Heartless creates a vessel, where the root of their being is placed. Their body becomes a construction or representation. If the vessel is damaged or destroyed, the Heartless practitioner is destroyed as well. Should

title: Pact Dice: The Practices (Wbow Version)
excerpt:
Conjure Schools

Conflict

War Magic

A predominant focus on combat magic, dueling, and, if there’s any utility to be had, controlling the battlefield. As a culture/group, war mages tend to focus on duels, with a mind to using them to

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — PACT DICE: INCARNATES
excerpt: Incarnate practitioners face down the pillars of reality. In the stories, a man may play chess with Death, a King may meet with the Fates, and people worry of the approach of Death, Conquest, Famine, and War. The Incarnate Practitioners

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — Being an Incarnate Practitioner

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — Example Incarnations

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — Basic Practice - Brand
excerpt:
A deceptively defensive practice due to the pause it may give others, Incarnate practitioners can draw power into a hand or tool. It becomes a heavy thing to bear, but gains the ability to mark an area… or a foe.

The Practice: The Brand

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — Basic Practice - Aspect
excerpt:
A beginner practice that teaches the management of the ‘pressure valve’ of an Incarnation. A sliver of the incarnation is brought forth and exerts its influence.

The Practice: A piece of poetry or something suitable to the Incarnation

title: Pact Dice: Incarnates — Basic Practice - Substantiated Word
excerpt: Incarnate practitioners build something when engaging regularly with Incarnations. Progress along tracks can be spent to get (oft cryptic) answers or make appointments to, for example, play a friendly game of chess with Death. This is more

title: Pact Dice: Harbinger — PACT DICE: Harbingers
excerpt: Chosen of a higher power or powerful other, who bears their strength & serves to clear the way, metaphorically. Tends to come with transformations, changes, costs in other forms, far more direct, and doesn't necessarily have (or rarely

title: Pact Dice: Harbinger — The Elements of a Harbinger
excerpt: Harbingers are inducted, a process that usually involves a deal and a mark. The nature of this deal, the mark, the means of binding and release and the power that they dealt with are all critically important. Harbingers are easily confused

title: Pact Dice: Harbinger — Harbinger Practices
excerpt:
Basic Practices

Fulmination (Missile) - Power of one’s storm is gathered up and concentrated into a projectile. Hurts, and on a direct hit, amps up the negative effects of the storm for the target. On a miss, still fills the area hit with

title: Pact Dice: Blood Magic — PACT DICE: BLOOD
excerpt: Sacrifice, one’s own blood being shed to empower effects, and especially the maturation of tools, such as knives that grow more dangerous for every life they take, kapalas that store spilled blood & keep it fresh for use at another time.

title: Pact Dice: Essentials — Foundations of the Practice
excerpt:
The root of the practice is one’s word. By undergoing the awakening ritual or otherwise agreeing to the broader compact with the spiritual forces driving the world, practitioners make their words and actions matter.

More to come.

title: Mile End: Dramatis Personae — The Lords of Montréal
excerpt:
The Montréaler

No contact - attendance at the councils meetings only. These meetings are once every month at one in the morning on the last Thursday of that month. One, two, or three may appear and will set the tone of the meeting.
